channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: People are continuing to park on the pavement, 23rd March
item.description:
The tanning shop on this location is encouraging people to park on the pavement.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 107, Main Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: RiotCop-Cycling in Prohibited pedestrian area Market Place., 23rd March
item.description:
Uncertain RiotPolice Cyclist:Broadway CT. (obo NHW), 1st entry here, so chose most apt category. Am unclear of IF, can report this particUlar incident here, however it overlaps into a general matter within signage remit/prohibited from cycling. Gentleman, rearView, shown in picture had zipped passing commuters awaiting bus, below the dedication of ClockTower (to the late HRM, Queen Elizabeth 2nd), at 0633gmt+ 23/03/2026, onwards reaching MPR-West, possibly heading towards Crayford. This time in morning is low number pedestrian usage, however the unique aspect being was wearing a non sanctioned Hi-Viz, emblazoned with 'Police', plus had as helmet, a specific Police Riot shield specification. Whilst, an officer must be encouraged to keep fitness levels up, am unsure weather it is okay for them to adorn for commuting to work in work-wear that's usually for specified purposes? Alternatively he's some form of wannabe cop or even actor who's unaware of the sanctions that they might incur, for impersonating an officer. Could.., possibly some extra efforts be placed into making the signs prohibiting cycling in pedestrian areas of the Broadway, made more prominent as many appear to flaut this rule.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 65, Broadway, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox on corner of drive, 23rd March
item.description:
Fox is in a black sack on the corner of the driver on okehampton Crescent
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Okehampton Crescent, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped rubbish, 23rd March
item.description:
Once again someone has decided to fly tip rubbish in this corner

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 22A,22B,22C,22D, Sholden Gardens, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Telecoms cabinet is knocked over, exposed wires, 23rd March
item.description:
For at least a week a telecoms cabinet has been knocked over. There are exposed wires as seen in the pictures which could be a risk to children. It's on Petersham Drive just before number 102.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Petersham Drive, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Utility Works and Temporary Traffic Lights

item.title: Large amounts of litter all along the road, 23rd March
item.description:
There is such a large amount of litter all along Heavitree Rd especially in the kerb. I’ve recently done a littler pick but there’s too much along here and it needs a proper sweep all along both sides. Can this be done please?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Heavitree Close, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumping of waste, 23rd March
item.description:
This waste has been left here for a while

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Commerell Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Smashed glass all over the pavement, 23rd March
item.description:
Smashed glass all over the pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sweet Briar Grove, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter all over street, 23rd March
item.description:
I have cleaned up as much as I can but see no point in paying council tax if the basics cannot be done by council. Every Friday rubbish is dumped on streets and then builds inducing the week. Having lived on Gilbert road for over 20 years the issue has progressively gotten worse. I have written to my MP to complain about the litter as I seem to complain ever other week but to no avail.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, Gilbert Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress and bags, 23rd March
item.description:
Fly tipping

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Needles and drug paraphernalia on stairs, 23rd March
item.description:
Stairs going down from flyover to alley behind station. Needles and drug paraphernalia on the stairs. Mess everywhere. So dangerous to walk with children.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 301, Lower Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish all over street, 23rd March
item.description:
Yet again I'm having to complain about the amount of rubbish on my road. I cannot recall the last time the road was swept. When bins are collected on Fridays more running is left on the pavement then is collected.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, Gilbert Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped, 23rd March
item.description:
Rubbish dumped and litter scattered across street
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sweet Briar Grove, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Truck full of waste abandon by trellick tower, 23rd March
item.description:
Blocking access to area

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elkstone Road, North Kensington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Very dangerous 15 inch pothole in Longlands Road, near the pavement.. ., 23rd March
item.description:
This pothole has been there for several weeks. I first saw it on 31 December 2025. It is now so deep that an inverted traffic cone has been put into it. It is near to the pavement, so is extremely dangerous both for traffic but also for pedestrians with mobility or sight issues. Also for those pushing buggies or prams. It is disgusting that this issue has not been rectified, as the problem has already been reported.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 277, Main Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping of mattress. Blocking access to rubbish bins, 23rd March
item.description:
This mattress has been dumped today on Portland Street close to 100-116 Portland Street. It’s blocking communal gates to rubbish and recycling bins.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Portland Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
